Empires & Puzzles Budatin Review: The 3-Star Gargoyle Strategy Guide

Budatin arrived with the Gargoyle family promising stone-hard defenses and mana manipulation. But does this Average speed 3-star actually deliver, or is he just a statue taking up roster space? We break down the stats, the skills, and why the community gave him such a cold reception.
Budatin: The Gargoyle Who Does... Well, Not Much
Budatin: The Gargoyle Who Waits... And Waits... For Tiles

Budatin is a 3-star Rare Dark (Purple) hero from the Sanctuary of Gargoyles event, first released in 2022. In a family known for incredibly annoying passive abilities and strong defensive stats, Budatin stands out as the odd one out. While his brethren are busy smashing enemies or overheating allies, Budatin is playing a tile-matching mini-game of his own. He is a "Board Alterer," a niche role that attempts to manipulate the flow of mana through shield enhancement.

Budatin embraces the classic grotesque aesthetic of the Gargoyle family. He looks like a mischievous imp caught in the middle of a prank, with horns, claws, and a tongue wagging out. The purple loincloth and glowing accents tie him firmly to the Dark element. It is a fitting design for a creature meant to perch on a cathedral and mock passersby, though in this case, he might be mocking the player trying to find a use for him.

Budatin possesses a defensive stat distribution. With 729 base HP and 502 Defense, he is quite sturdy for a 3-star hero. When Limit Broken (LB1 and LB2), these numbers balloon significantly, making him a tough nut to crack, especially when combined with his passive Stoneskin. However, his Attack stat is on the lower side, which reinforces his role as a support or tank character rather than a damage dealer. In the modern meta, his defensive bulk holds up well, but his low attack power makes him less threatening in tile damage scenarios compared to newer 3-star attackers.
This skill is... puzzling. "Abiding Dogma" alters the power of all Dark shields on the board.
When you cast it, existing Dark shields give +37% more mana.
When he casts it, existing Dark shields give attackers -30% less mana.

On defense, he can act as a mana bump for the attacker if they are using purple, or a slight annoyance, but he poses zero immediate threat to the attacker's health pool.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Gargoyle family bonus provides immunity to status ailments from specific elements based on the number of Gargoyles in the team.

Immune to Poison, Burn, and Water damage.
This is excellent for 3-star tournaments where damage-over-time (DoT) heroes like Tyrum, Jahangir, or Waqas are common. Being immune to Poison alone makes him a counter to several popular 3-stars.
This is the saving grace of the entire family. After firing his special, Budatin reduces all damage from the next 4 hits to 1.
In a 3-star setting, this makes him incredibly durable. Multi-hitters or slash attacks just bounce off him.
he fires. At Average speed, he might die before getting his stone armor up.
Reduces damage received for the first few turns. This synergizes well with his role as a meat shield, ensuring he survives long enough to hopefully fire his special and activate Stoneskin.
Upon release, Budatin was immediately identified as one of the weakest heroes in the Gargoyle lineup. Players compared him unfavorably to nearly every other Dark 3-star, from Tyrum to Treevil.

While his stats are decent thanks to the Gargoyle baseline, his skill has aged like milk—or rather, it was sour from the start. Newer 3-star heroes from events like Opera, Goblins, or Construct offer massive damage, healing, or game-changing ailments at Faster speeds.

Budatin is largely irrelevant in today's competitive 3-star meta. He is overshadowed by Treevil (Very Slow but game-ending), Maeve (Fast defense down), and essentially any sniper who actually removes an enemy from the field. His only niche is perhaps as a tank in a Rush tournament if you literally have no other options, simply because his Stoneskin makes him annoying to kill.
Maximizes his only real asset: his bulk. Keeps him alive longer to trigger Stoneskin and the family bonus.

Increases tile damage, which is the only way he contributes to killing anything.
He remains Average speed and does no skill damage, so this feels like a waste.
path is recommended. If you are forced to use him, you want him to sit there and soak up damage while your other heroes do the actual work.
Reducing damage to 1 for 4 hits is undeniably strong survival tech.
Immune to Poison (and potentially Burn/Water) which counters specific 3-star threats.
His special skill does no damage, no healing, and applies no status effects to heroes.
Requires specific tile conditions on the board to provide any value.
Too slow for a setup hero that needs to fire
He relies entirely on the board and his teammates to win. He cannot turn a losing match around.
His speed becomes Very Fast. He can fire early, get Stoneskin up, and potentially mess with the attacker's mana gain from tiles.
Can be used in events purely to soak up hits thanks to Stoneskin if you need to protect a glass cannon.
Budatin - Treevil - Maeve - C. Tyrum - C. Rigard

If you lack other Dark boosters like Sergei or Khonshu, Budatin can technically increase mana gain from tiles to help recharge your slow hitters like Treevil. It is a desperate move, but mathematically valid.
A stall defense for Rush tournaments. The goal here is just to not die. Budatin sits there activating Stoneskin while Treevil inevitably kills everyone.
His Stoneskin is undispellable, which is a plus.
Since he deals no damage, he cannot clear minions. Kvasir shuts him down by simply ignoring him.
Smart attackers will dump non-Purple tiles into him to charge their heroes, knowing he poses no threat when he fires.

If you pull Budatin early in your game, do not prioritize him. Standard Season 1 heroes like Tyrum, Balthazar, or even Renfeld (especially with costume) offer more utility and direct impact. Keep him only if you have absolutely no other Dark 3-stars or need a Gargoyle for the family bonus.

He is "food" (leveling fodder) or a museum piece. Even in the deepest rosters for Tower events or Contest of Elements, it is hard to justify a slot for him. There are simply too many 3-star heroes who actively contribute to winning the match. Do not waste Alpha Aethers here.
The community reaction to Budatin was famously harsh.
One player on the forum summed it up perfectly:
"He fires his special which does no damage, no heal, no minion, no status effect, no buff, no dispel, no cleanse, but he does get to fire his special again faster!"
"I think the developer for this particular hero was on coffee break when they snatched this unfinished concept off his desk."
"Even at Very Fast it would be crap. But I would keep one until the inevitable buff..."
The polls were brutal, with the vast majority voting "Not at all" for summoning and "Food" for his primary purpose.

Budatin is a fascinating study in game design—a hero with a unique mechanic that fails completely in practice due to speed and board RNG. He is a gargoyle that forgot to be scary. While his passive Stoneskin is a top-tier defensive ability, it is wasted on a hero who exerts no pressure on the enemy. You can safely leave him on the bench, or better yet, the Soul Exchange (if 3-stars were allowed).
A stone-cold pass. Keep one for the collection if you must, but don't expect him to fly.
